items.headline: City launches business license amnesty program for local shops
items.summary: Cupertino is offering an amnesty program to help unlicensed businesses at shopping centers get compliant without penalties. The city is also rolling out a 'Shop Local' campaign and new economic incentives to support retail and small businesses.

items.headline: New mixed-use residential development approved at 490 East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project at 490 East Middlefield Road on the second reading, meaning the project is moving forward. This will bring new housing and likely ground-floor commercial space to the area.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive grant funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for California's Prohousing Incentive Program (Round 4) to secure state funding for housing initiatives. If approved, this could help accelerate housing development and affordability efforts in Mountain View.

items.headline: Santa Clara opens public comment on 2026-27 housing grant spending plan
items.summary: The city is accepting public feedback on how it will use federal HUD grant money for housing in the coming year. This is your chance to weigh in on housing priorities before the plan is finalized.

items.headline: City holds public meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale held a community meeting on March 23 to discuss a safe parking initiative. This program typically addresses homelessness and vehicle living situations in the city.
